news.title:
ABB India released its 76th AGM notice and Integrated Annual Report 2025, showcasing sustainability and performance highlights.
Financials for CY 2025: orders at ₹14,115 Cr (+8%), revenues at ₹13,203 Cr (+8%), PAT at ₹1,669 Cr (-12%).
news.summary:
ABB India released its 76th AGM notice and Integrated Annual Report 2025, showcasing sustainability and performance highlights.
Financials for CY 2025: orders at ₹14,115 Cr (+8%), revenues at ₹13,203 Cr (+8%), PAT at ₹1,669 Cr (-12%).

news.title:
India's grid modernization, under RDSS, faces execution delays but shows progress, with AT&C losses dropping from 21.9% in FY21 to 16.2% in FY25.
Stocks like Hitachi Energy, Siemens, and ABB India benefit from high-spec hardware demand, while Genus Power and HPL Electric face cash flow risks.
news.summary:
India's grid modernization, under RDSS, faces execution delays but shows progress, with AT&C losses dropping from 21.9% in FY21 to 16.2% in FY25.
Stocks like Hitachi Energy, Siemens, and ABB India benefit from high-spec hardware demand, while Genus Power and HPL Electric face cash flow risks.

news.title:
ABB India Ltd to hold board meeting on May 8, 2026, for Q1 FY26 results.
Trading window for ABB India's securities closed from Mar 15 to May 10, 2026.
news.summary:
ABB India Ltd to hold board meeting on May 8, 2026, for Q1 FY26 results.
Trading window for ABB India's securities closed from Mar 15 to May 10, 2026.

news.title:
ABB India rose 3.46%, reaching Rs 6,189.85, outperforming Sensex's 2.52 gain.
Despite underperforming its sector by 1.86%, ABB India faces 5-day moving average resistance.
news.summary:
ABB India rose 3.46%, reaching Rs 6,189.85, outperforming Sensex's 2.52 gain.
Despite underperforming its sector by 1.86%, ABB India faces 5-day moving average resistance.

news.title:
ABB India Ltd received a Customs order with a penalty of Rs. 17.7L and fine of Rs. 1.8L.
The company plans to appeal the order and expects no material financial impact.
news.summary:
ABB India Ltd received a Customs order with a penalty of Rs. 17.7L and fine of Rs. 1.8L.
The company plans to appeal the order and expects no material financial impact.

news.title:
ABB India opened 3.76% higher on 24 Mar 2026, signaling strong momentum.
The heavy electrical equipment sector benefits from a broadly favorable market.
news.summary:
ABB India opened 3.76% higher on 24 Mar 2026, signaling strong momentum.
The heavy electrical equipment sector benefits from a broadly favorable market.

news.title: ABB India shares increase over 1% after UBS upgrades rating to buy.
news.summary: ABB India shares increase over 1% after UBS upgrades rating to buy.

news.title:
UBS upgrades ABB India to Buy from Neutral, raising TP to ₹8,030.
Upgrade reflects optimism on ABB India's growth and earnings potential.
news.summary:
UBS upgrades ABB India to Buy from Neutral, raising TP to ₹8,030.

news.title: ABB India rebounds 4% from opening levels, gaining nearly 2%.

news.title:
ABB India rose 3.3% intraday on 10 Mar 2026, hitting Rs 6,178.05.
It outperformed Sensex's 0.55% gain, leading the Heavy Electrical Equipment sector.
news.summary:
ABB India rose 3.3% intraday on 10 Mar 2026, hitting Rs 6,178.05.

news.title: ABB India plans $75M investment in manufacturing & R&D by 2026.
news.summary: ABB India plans $75M investment in manufacturing & R&D by 2026.

news.title:
ABB India's Dec 2025 net sales rose 5.7% Y-o-Y to Rs 3,557.01 cr; net profit fell 18.1% to Rs 432.85 cr.
EBITDA dropped 16.3% Y-o-Y to Rs 622.58 cr; EPS decreased to Rs 20.43 from Rs 24.93 in Dec 2024.
news.summary:
ABB India's Dec 2025 net sales rose 5.7% Y-o-Y to Rs 3,557.01 cr; net profit fell 18.1% to Rs 432.85 cr.
EBITDA dropped 16.3% Y-o-Y to Rs 622.58 cr; EPS decreased to Rs 20.43 from Rs 24.93 in Dec 2024.

news.title:
ABB India reported robust Q4 CY2025 results with highest-ever orders of INR 14,115 crore, 8% growth YoY, and revenue of INR 13,203 crore.
Sustainability initiatives included AWS Gold Certification, while strong order momentum emerged from transport, infrastructure, and renewables sectors.
news.summary:
ABB India reported robust Q4 CY2025 results with highest-ever orders of INR 14,115 crore, 8% growth YoY, and revenue of INR 13,203 crore.
Sustainability initiatives included AWS Gold Certification, while strong order momentum emerged from transport, infrastructure, and renewables sectors.

news.title:
ABB India executed a Rs. 29.1 crore NSE block trade for 47,858 shares.
The shares were priced at Rs. 6083.5 each in the transaction.
news.summary:
ABB India executed a Rs. 29.1 crore NSE block trade for 47,858 shares.

news.title: ABB revises profit margin forecast from 16.9% to 12-15%, indicating challenges.
news.summary: ABB revises profit margin forecast from 16.9% to 12-15%, indicating challenges.

news.title:
ABB India management sees positive demand outlook with market revival in 2026.
The revival is expected to be driven by private capital expenditures.

news.title:
ABB India targets 12-15% PAT margins and double-digit revenue growth.

news.title:
ABB's Q4 EBITDA margin rose 1.7% due to forex and commodity gains.
Forex gain in Q4CY25 was ₹62 crore, versus a ₹40 crore loss previously.
news.summary:
ABB's Q4 EBITDA margin rose 1.7% due to forex and commodity gains.
